(born 13 August 1991)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a Scottish international [lawn and indoor bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls").
["2016 World Bowls Championship Finals"](http://www.burnsidebowlingclub.com/2016WorldBowlsChampionshipFinalsResults.html). Burnside Bowling Club.
["Second World Indoor title as Jamie Chestney gains revenge over Darren Burnett"](https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/bowls/42793074). BBC Sport.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Scottish bowls player

Lesley Doig

| Personal information | |
| --- | --- |
| Nationality | British (Scottish) |
| Born | (1991-08-13) 13 August 1991 (age 34)  [Perth, Scotland](/wiki/Perth,_Scotland "Perth, Scotland") |
| Sport | |
| Sport | [Lawn & indoor bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") |
| Club | Strathmiglo BC |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | [World Outdoor Championships](/wiki/World_Bowls_Championship "World Bowls Championship") | | | | Silver medal – second place | [2016 Christchurch](/wiki/2016_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2016 World Outdoor Bowls Championship") | [singles](/wiki/2016_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_-_Women%27s_Singles "2016 World Outdoor Bowls Championship - Women's Singles") | | Bronze medal – third place | [2016 Christchurch](/wiki/2016_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2016 World Outdoor Bowls Championship") | [pairs](/wiki/2016_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_-_Women%27s_Pairs "2016 World Outdoor Bowls Championship - Women's Pairs") | | [World Indoor Bowls Championships](/wiki/World_Indoor_Bowls_Championships "World Indoor Bowls Championships") | | | | Gold medal – first place | [2018 Yarmouth](/wiki/2018_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2018 World Indoor Bowls Championship") | [Mixed pairs](/wiki/2018_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2018 World Indoor Bowls Championship") | | Bronze medal – third place | [2018 Gold Coast](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games "2018 Commonwealth Games") | [Pairs](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games_-_Women%27s_pairs "Lawn bowls at the 2018 Commonwealth Games - Women's pairs") | | |

**Lesley Jayne Doig** (born 13 August 1991)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a Scottish international [lawn and indoor bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls").

Doig won a bronze medal in the 2015 World Junior Championship before achieving a notable success by winning the silver medal in the [singles](/wiki/2016_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_-_Women%27s_Singles "2016 World Outdoor Bowls Championship - Women's Singles") at the [2016 World Outdoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2016_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2016 World Outdoor Bowls Championship") in [Christchurch](/wiki/Christchurch "Christchurch") losing out to [Karen Murphy](/wiki/Karen_Murphy_(lawn_bowls) "Karen Murphy (lawn bowls)") in the final.[[2]](#cite_note-2) She then won the [bronze medal](/wiki/Bronze_medal "Bronze medal") in the [pairs](/wiki/2016_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_-_Women%27s_Pairs "2016 World Outdoor Bowls Championship - Women's Pairs") with [Lauren Baillie](/wiki/Lauren_Baillie "Lauren Baillie").

In 2018, Doig won the [World Indoor Mixed Pairs title](/wiki/2018_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2018 World Indoor Bowls Championship") with [Jamie Chestney](/wiki/Jamie_Chestney "Jamie Chestney").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

She was selected as part of the [Scottish team](/wiki/Scotland_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Scotland at the 2018 Commonwealth Games") for the 2018 [Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Commonwealth_Games "Commonwealth Games") on the [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Gold Coast, Queensland") in [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland")[[4]](#cite_note-4) where she won a [bronze medal](/wiki/Bronze_medal "Bronze medal") in the [Pairs](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_pairs "Lawn bowls at the 2018 Commonwealth Games – Women's pairs") with [Claire Johnston](/wiki/Claire_Johnston_(bowls) "Claire Johnston (bowls)").[[5]](#cite_note-5)
